title: 10 best herbal teas for cold sore
intro_desc: Herbal teas for cold sores are natural remedies made from various herbs that can help alleviate the symptoms of cold sores. These teas can reduce inflammation, boost the immune system, and promote healing. Some examples of herbal teas that can help with cold sores include chamomile, lemon balm, and echinacea tea. Chamomile tea has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce redness and swelling, while lemon balm tea contains compounds that can inhibit the herpes simplex virus that causes cold sores. Echinacea tea can boost the immune system and help the body fight off the virus. By drinking these herbal teas, individuals can find relief from the pain and discomfort associated with cold sores and improve their overall quality of life.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Pot marigold tea helps with cold sores because it contains anti-viral, anti-inflammatory, and anti-bacterial properties that can help reduce the severity and duration of cold sores. The tea's active compounds, such as flavonoids and carotenoids, can help to inhibit the replication of the herpes simplex virus, which is responsible for cold sores. Additionally, the tea's anti-inflammatory properties can help to reduce redness, swelling, and pain associated with cold sores. Drinking pot marigold tea or applying it topically as a compress may provide relief from cold sore symptoms.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Pot marigold tea helps reduce inflammation and swelling associated with cold sores, thanks to its high flavonoid and saponin content.
Antiviral: The tea's antiviral properties, derived from its flavonoid and alkaloid constituents, inhibit the herpes simplex virus responsible for cold sores.
Antioxidant: Pot marigold tea's rich antioxidant properties, due to its carotenoid and phenolic acid content, protect cells from damage and support cold sore healing.
Antimicrobial: The tea's antimicrobial effects, attributed to its alkaloid and flavonoid constituents, help prevent secondary bacterial infections during cold sore outbreaks.
Analgesic: Pot marigold tea's analgesic properties, resulting from its flavonoid and terpene content, alleviate pain and discomfort associated with cold sores.
remedy_recipe: In a stove-top pot, combine 2 cups of water and 2 tablespoons of dried pot marigold flowers.
Bring the mixture to a boil, then reduce heat and let it simmer for 10 minutes.
Strain the tea through a fine-mesh sieve into a cup, discarding the spent flowers.
Allow the tea to cool to a drinkable temperature.
Sip the tea slowly, ideally consuming the entire 2 cups within 2-3 hours.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: White tea helps with cold sores because it is rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory properties that can reduce redness, swelling, and pain associated with cold sores. The tea's antiviral properties may also help prevent the cold sore virus from replicating, which can shorten the duration of an outbreak. Additionally, white tea's high tannin content can help dry out the cold sore, which can help it heal faster. Drinking white tea or applying it topically can both be effective in treating cold sores. However, it is important to note that white tea should not be used as a substitute for medical treatment, especially in severe cases.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: White tea has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce redness, swelling, and pain associated with cold sores, due to the presence of flavonoids and polyphenols.

Antiviral: White tea contains antiviral properties that can help prevent the herpes simplex virus from replicating, thus reducing the severity and duration of cold sores, due to the presence of catechins and tannins.

Antioxidant: White tea has antioxidant properties that can help protect the skin from damage caused by free radicals, which can exacerbate cold sore symptoms, due to the presence of flavonoids and polyphenols.
Analgesic: White tea has analgesic properties that can help alleviate pain and discomfort caused by cold sores, due to the presence of flavonoids and polyphenols.

Immune-boosting: White tea has immune-boosting properties that can help strengthen the body's defenses against the herpes simplex virus, thus reducing the frequency and severity of cold sore outbreaks, due to the presence of flavonoids and polyphenols.
remedy_recipe: In a pot, pour 1 cup of water and bring it to a boil.
Add 1 teaspoon of dried white tea leaves and 1 teaspoon of dried lemon balm leaves to the boiling water.
Reduce heat to low, cover the pot and let it simmer for 5 minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup and let it cool down to room temperature.
Apply the tea to the cold sore using a clean cotton ball, leaving it on for 10 minutes before rinsing off.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: German chamomile tea helps with cold sores because it contains anti-inflammatory and antiviral properties that can reduce the severity and duration of cold sores. Chamomile's anti-inflammatory properties help soothe the pain and inflammation associated with cold sores, while its antiviral properties can help prevent the spread of the virus that causes cold sores. Drinking chamomile tea or applying chamomile tea bags to the affected area may provide relief from cold sore symptoms. Additionally, chamomile tea is rich in antioxidants, which can help boost the immune system and promote healing.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: German chamomile tea contains active constituents like chamazulene and bisabolol, which help reduce inflammation and swelling, providing relief from cold sore-related pain and discomfort.
Antiviral: The tea's antiviral properties, due to the presence of flavonoids and essential oils, can help inhibit the herpes simplex virus from replicating, thus preventing cold sore outbreaks.
Antioxidant: German chamomile tea is rich in antioxidants, such as terpenoids and flavonoids, that can help protect skin cells from oxidative stress and promote faster healing of cold sores.

Antimicrobial: The tea's antimicrobial properties, derived from its essential oils and active compounds, can help prevent secondary bacterial infections and promote a healthier environment for cold sore healing.

Analgesic: German chamomile tea contains analgesic constituents, including azulene and bisabolol, that can help alleviate pain, itching, and irritation associated with cold sores, providing comfort and relief.
remedy_recipe: Boil 1 cup of water and let it cool down for 2-3 minutes.
Add 1 tablespoon of dried German chamomile flowers to a tea infuser.
Pour the hot water over the chamomile flowers and let it steep for 5-10 minutes.
Strain the tea and discard the chamomile flowers.
Drink the tea while it's still warm, 2-3 times a day.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Eastern purple coneflower tea helps with cold sores because it contains anti-inflammatory and antiviral properties that can reduce the pain, swelling, and redness associated with cold sores. The tea is rich in echinacea, which boosts the immune system and helps the body fight off the virus that causes cold sores. Drinking the tea or applying it topically to the affected area can help speed up the healing process and prevent future outbreaks. Additionally, the tea's anti-inflammatory properties can help reduce the severity and duration of cold sore symptoms.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Eastern purple coneflower tea helps reduce inflammation and swelling associated with cold sores, thanks to its active constituents like eudesmanolides and germacranolides.
Antiviral: The tea has antiviral properties that can inhibit the herpes simplex virus (HSV-1) responsible for cold sores, due to the presence of active compounds such as isoprunietin and luteolin.
Analgesic: Eastern purple coneflower tea can alleviate pain caused by cold sores, as it contains active constituents like caffeic acid and chlorogenic acid that have analgesic effects.
Antioxidant: The tea's high antioxidant content, including flavonoids and phenolic acids, helps protect skin cells from damage and supports faster healing of cold sores.

Immune-boosting: Eastern purple coneflower tea promotes a healthy immune response, which is crucial for preventing cold sore outbreaks, due to its active constituents like alkamides and polyacetylenes.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 2-3 purple coneflower (Echinacea purpurea) flower heads, cutting them from the stalks with sharp scissors.
Rinse the flower heads under cool running water, gently patting them dry with a clean towel.
Coarsely chop the flower heads, placing them in a heat-proof teapot or infuser.
Pour 1 cup (8-12 fl. oz.) of boiling water over the chopped flower heads, allowing the tea to steep for 10-15 minutes.
Strain the tea, discarding the spent flower heads, and drink the tea while warm, up to three times a day for cold sore relief.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Licorice tea helps with cold sore because it contains a compound called glycyrrhizin, which has antiviral and anti-inflammatory properties. Glycyrrhizin can help reduce the pain and inflammation associated with cold sores, as well as inhibit the replication of the herpes simplex virus, which causes cold sores. Additionally, licorice tea can help boost the immune system, which can help the body fight off the virus and speed up the healing process. Drinking licorice tea or applying licorice tea bags topically to the affected area may provide relief and help reduce the severity and duration of cold sores.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Licorice tea's anti-inflammatory property, due to its active constituents like glycyrrhizin and flavonoids, helps reduce redness, swelling, and pain associated with cold sores.
Antiviral: The antiviral property of licorice tea, attributed to its glycyrrhizin content, inhibits the replication of the herpes simplex virus, causing cold sores.
Immune-boosting: Licorice tea's immune-boosting property, owing to its rich antioxidant content, strengthens the body's defense mechanisms, aiding in faster cold sore healing.
Demulcent: Licorice tea's demulcent property, resulting from its mucilage content, forms a protective layer on cold sores, reducing irritation and promoting tissue repair.
Analgesic: The analgesic property of licorice tea, attributed to its glycyrrhizin and flavonoids, alleviates pain and discomfort associated with cold sores, offering relief.
remedy_recipe: In a pot, combine 1 cup of water and 2 teaspoons of dried licorice root.
Bring the mixture to a boil, then reduce heat and let it simmer for 10 minutes.
Strain the tea to remove the licorice root pieces.
Allow the tea to cool down to a drinkable temperature.
Sip the tea slowly, aiming to finish it within 30 minutes.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Witch hazel tea helps with cold sore because it contains tannins, which have astringent properties that can help reduce inflammation and dry out the cold sore. The tea also has antioxidants and antimicrobial properties that can help protect the skin and promote healing. Drinking witch hazel tea may also boost the immune system, helping the body fight off the virus that causes cold sores. However, it is important to note that while witch hazel tea may help alleviate symptoms, it is not a cure for cold sores.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Witch hazel tea reduces inflammation and swelling, providing relief from cold sore pain and discomfort. This property is important for cold sores because it helps alleviate the painful symptoms associated with the condition. The active constituents responsible for this property are tannins and gallic acid.

Astringent: Witch hazel tea tightens and tones skin, helping to dry out cold sores and promote healing. This property is important for cold sores because it helps to reduce the duration and severity of outbreaks. The active constituents responsible for this property are tannins and gallic acid.

Antiviral: Witch hazel tea inhibits the replication of the herpes simplex virus, preventing the spread of cold sores. This property is important for cold sores because it helps to stop the virus from multiplying and causing further outbreaks. The active constituents responsible for this property are gallic acid and catechins.

Antioxidant: Witch hazel tea protects skin cells from damage, promoting healing and reducing the risk of scarring. This property is important for cold sores because it helps to support the skin's natural healing process and prevent long-term damage. The active constituents responsible for this property are proanthocyanidins and flavonoids.

Analgesic: Witch hazel tea reduces pain and itching, providing relief from cold sore symptoms. This property is important for cold sores because it helps to alleviate the discomfort and irritation associated with the condition. The active constituents responsible for this property are tannins and volatile oils.
remedy_recipe: In a stovetop kettle, boil 1 cup of water for 5-7 minutes.
Pour the hot water into a teapot, then add 1 tablespoon of dried witch hazel leaves.
Allow the tea to steep for 10-15 minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup, discarding the spent leaves.
Sip the warm tea slowly, aiming to finish it all within 30 minutes. Repeat this process 2-3 times a day for cold sore relief.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: English ivy tea helps with cold sore because it contains anti-viral and anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce the severity and duration of cold sores. The ivy's active compounds, such as saponins and flavonoids, can inhibit the replication of the herpes simplex virus, which is responsible for causing cold sores. Additionally, the tea's anti-inflammatory properties can help alleviate pain, swelling, and redness associated with cold sores. Drinking English ivy tea or applying it topically to the affected area can provide relief and promote healing.
remedy_properties: Anti-viral: English ivy tea contains triterpenoid saponins, which have been shown to have anti-viral properties, helping to prevent the herpes simplex virus from replicating and causing cold sores.
Anti-inflammatory: English ivy tea has anti-inflammatory properties due to its flavonoid content, which can help reduce redness, swelling, and pain associated with cold sores.
Antioxidant: English ivy tea contains high levels of antioxidants, which can help protect skin cells from damage caused by free radicals, promoting healing and reducing the severity of cold sores.
Astringent: English ivy tea contains tannins, which have astringent properties, helping to dry out cold sores and promote faster healing.
Analgesic: English ivy tea contains various compounds, such as saponins and flavonoids, that have analgesic properties, helping to alleviate pain and discomfort associated with cold sores.
remedy_recipe: Pick three English ivy leaves, ensuring they are fully grown and healthy.
Rinse the leaves thoroughly under cold water to remove dirt and bacteria.
Boil 2 cups of water and steep the leaves for 10 minutes to release the active compounds.
Strain the tea to remove leaves and let it cool down to a drinkable temperature.
Drink the tea twice a day until cold sores disappear, typically within a few days.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: St. John's wort tea helps with cold sore because it contains anti-viral and anti-inflammatory properties that can reduce the severity and duration of cold sores. The herb's active compounds, such as hypericin and hyperforin, have been shown to inhibit the replication of the herpes simplex virus, which causes cold sores. Additionally, St. John's wort tea can help alleviate the pain, itching, and inflammation associated with cold sores. Drinking the tea or applying it topically to the affected area may provide relief and promote healing. However, it is important to note that St. John's wort can interact with certain medications, so it is always best to consult with a healthcare provider before using it as a treatment for cold sores.
remedy_properties: Antiviral property: St. John's Wort tea contains hypericin and hyperforin, which have antiviral properties that can help reduce the severity and duration of cold sores by inhibiting the replication of the herpes simplex virus.
Anti-inflammatory property: The tea has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce redness, swelling, and pain associated with cold sores, making it easier to manage symptoms.

Antioxidant property: St. John's Wort tea contains flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have antioxidant properties that can help protect skin cells from damage caused by free radicals, promoting healing and reducing the risk of cold sore recurrence.
Antimicrobial property: The tea has antimicrobial properties that can help prevent secondary infections caused by bacteria, reducing the risk of complications and promoting faster healing.

Nervine property: St. John's Wort tea has nervine properties that can help reduce anxiety and stress, which can trigger cold sore outbreaks. The tea can help promote relaxation and a sense of calm, reducing the likelihood of cold sore recurrence.
remedy_recipe: In a teapot, combine 1 teaspoon of dried St. John's Wort herb with 8 ounces of boiling water.
Allow the herb to steep in the water for 10-15 minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup, discarding the used herb.
Let the tea cool to a comfortable drinking temperature.
Sip the tea slowly, aiming to consume the entire 8 ounces.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: English lavender tea helps with cold sores because it contains anti-inflammatory and antiviral properties that can reduce inflammation and inhibit the herpes simplex virus from replicating. The tea's antioxidants can also help boost the immune system and promote healing. Drinking lavender tea or applying it topically to cold sores may help alleviate symptoms and speed up recovery time. Additionally, lavender's soothing and calming effects can help reduce stress and anxiety, which can trigger cold sore outbreaks. Overall, English lavender tea can provide a natural and holistic approach to managing cold sores.
remedy_properties: Antiviral property: English lavender tea contains compounds such as linalool and linalyl acetate, which have been shown to have antiviral properties that can help reduce the severity and duration of cold sores by inhibiting the herpes simplex virus responsible for causing cold sores.

Anti-inflammatory property: The anti-inflammatory properties of English lavender tea, attributed to compounds like cineole and flavonoids, can help alleviate pain, swelling, and redness associated with cold sores, thus promoting faster healing.

Antioxidant property: English lavender tea is rich in antioxidants, such as rosmarinic acid and apigenin, which can help protect the skin from oxidative stress and damage, supporting the immune system and facilitating the healing of cold sores.

Analgesic property: The analgesic properties of English lavender tea, due to compounds like borneol and geraniol, can help relieve pain and discomfort caused by cold sores, making it easier to manage symptoms.

Anxiolytic property: English lavender tea has anxiolytic properties, attributed to compounds like linalool and lavandulol, which can help reduce anxiety and stress, promoting overall well-being and supporting the immune system in fighting off cold sores.
remedy_recipe: In a small pot, boil 1 cup of water for 3-5 minutes.
Add 1 teaspoon of dried English lavender flowers to the boiling water.
Steep the lavender in the hot water for 5-10 minutes.
Strain out the lavender flowers, leaving only the tea.
Drink the lavender tea while it is still warm, up to 3 times a day.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemon balm tea helps with cold sore because it contains antiviral properties that can help reduce the severity and duration of cold sores. The tea's active compounds, including rosmarinic acid and flavonoids, have been shown to inhibit the herpes simplex virus, which is responsible for cold sores. Drinking lemon balm tea or applying it topically can help reduce inflammation, redness, and pain associated with cold sores. Additionally, lemon balm has a calming effect on the nervous system, which can help alleviate stress and anxiety that can trigger cold sore outbreaks. Overall, lemon balm tea is a natural and effective remedy for managing cold sores.
remedy_properties: Antiviral: Lemon balm tea contains active constituents like rosmarinic acid and flavonoids that have antiviral properties, which can help prevent the herpes simplex virus from replicating and thus reduce the severity and duration of cold sores.
Anti-inflammatory: Lemon balm tea has anti-inflammatory properties due to its rosmarinic acid content, which can help alleviate pain, swelling, and redness associated with cold sores.
Antioxidant: Lemon balm tea is rich in antioxidants, such as phenolic acids and flavonoids, which can help protect skin cells from oxidative damage and support the healing process of cold sores.
Analgesic: Lemon balm tea contains compounds like eugenol and tannins that have analgesic properties, which can help relieve pain and discomfort caused by cold sores.

Immune-boosting: Lemon balm tea has immune-boosting properties thanks to its rosmarinic acid and eugenol content, which can help strengthen the body's defenses and promote faster recovery from cold sores.
remedy_recipe: Pick 2 tablespoons of fresh lemon balm leaves or use 1 tablespoon of dried leaves.
Rinse the lemon balm leaves under cold water to remove any dirt or impurities.
Place the leaves in a teapot and pour 1 cup of boiling water over them.
Let the tea steep for 5-10 minutes to allow the herb's properties to be released.
Strain the tea and drink it while it's still warm, up to 3 times a day.

supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat cold sore with herbal teas is by using chamomile or licorice tea. Soak a cotton ball in the cooled tea and apply it directly to the cold sore. Chamomile has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce redness and swelling, while licorice contains a compound called glycyrrhizin that has antiviral properties and can help speed up the healing process. Drinking these teas can also provide internal benefits and strengthen the immune system. Remember to always consult with a healthcare professional before starting any new treatment.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to cold sores that are treated with herbal teas include canker sores and fever blisters. These conditions can cause discomfort and pain, similar to cold sores. Herbal teas that contain ingredients such as chamomile, licorice root, and echinacea can help to soothe and heal these ailments. Chamomile has anti-inflammatory properties that can reduce redness and swelling, while licorice root contains compounds that can help to speed up the healing process. Echinacea, on the other hand, can boost the immune system and help the body to fight off infections. Drinking these herbal teas can provide relief from the symptoms of cold sores, canker sores, and fever blisters, and can help to promote healing. However, it is important to consult with a healthcare professional before using herbal teas as a treatment for these ailments.
